name: Bikaner
description: Bikaner is a magnificent destination in Rajasthan, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Bikaner offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Bikaner boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Junagarh Fort
topAttractions.description: One of the finest forts in Rajasthan (1593 CE, built by Raja Rai Singh), never conquered despite multiple attacks. 37 palaces, temples, and pavilions inside the 5.28-acre complex — Anup Mahal (red sandstone with gold filigree), Phool Mahal, Badal Mahal, and the Karan Mahal. The Indian Air Force museum section has a vintage WWII biplane. Entry: ₹100 (Indian), ₹500 (foreign).

topAttractions.name: Karni Mata Temple (Rat Temple)
topAttractions.description: A revered temple 30km south at Deshnok dedicated to Karni Mata (a 15th-century mystic). Over 25,000 rats (kabas) live here and are considered sacred — believed to be reincarnated devotees awaiting rebirth as humans. White rats are especially auspicious. Pilgrims walk barefoot on rat-infested floors. Entry: ₹10 (Indian), ₹50 (foreign, without camera).

topAttractions.name: Lalgarh Palace
topAttractions.description: A magnificent Indo-Saracenic palace complex built by Maharaja Ganga Singh in 1902 using local pink sandstone and Belgian glass. Now partly a heritage hotel (Laxmi Niwas Hotel), partly the Sadul Museum with royal portraits, hunting trophies, and vintage photographs.

topAttractions.name: National Research Centre on Camel
topAttractions.description: India's only camel research center, 8km from Bikaner — home to 300+ camels of different breeds including the Bikaneri breed (best for carrying loads). Camel rides, camel milk tasting (₹30/glass, claimed to have medicinal properties). A unique and educational visit.

topAttractions.name: Bikaneri Bhujia & Food Trail
topAttractions.description: Bikaner is the birthplace of Bhujia namkeen — crispy spiced moth bean noodles that became a national snack. Original Haldiram's started here. The Kote Gate area has dozens of competing bhujia manufacturers. Visit the old city bhujia markets and buy directly from traditional makers (₹150-500/kg, far better than packaged).

topAttractions.name: Gajner Palace & Gajner Wildlife Sanctuary
topAttractions.description: A 32km drive from Bikaner brings you to this former Maharaja of Bikaner hunting lodge (1884) on the shores of Gajner Lake — now a heritage hotel. The Gajner Wildlife Sanctuary has blackbuck, chinkara, and wild boar. The lake attracts imperial sandgrouse (thousands in winter) and flamingos.

culture: The culture of Bikaner is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
